reasoning: Recent data up to late October 2025 shows the series hovering around 1055-1056 billion, exhibiting a very shallow upward linear trend (about +0.078 billion per day) but significant week-to-week volatility (+/- 5 billion). The FRED statistical analysis estimates a 55% probability of a short-term increase passing the recent threshold value for the nearest resolution date in November. Given the noise in the seasonally adjusted series and unpredictable short-to-medium-term credit tightness (tightened standards noted as recently as the start of the year), the probabilities remain close to a coin flip. Following the forecasting principles, the probability of an increase starts slightly above 50% given the historical expansionary trend and incrementally converges to 0.5 as the forecast horizon extends to November 25 and late January 2026.
